history_text: 38-year-old male with a 3-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently well-controlled (HbA1c 6.7%). Comorbidities include T2DM with hyperglycaemia, peripheral neuropathy, stage 3 chronic kidney disease. Renal function is mildly to moderately reduced (eGFR 60.0 mL/min/1.73m²). Current medications include Insulin NPH 22 units nightly, Amlodipine 5mg once daily, Omeprazole 20mg once daily. No known drug allergies. He denies recent hospitalisations or surgical procedures.
